artPrompt: An evocative scene of a great open ledger floating in a smoky midnight chapel, its blank illegible pages glowing as ghostly coins and dice tumble in and ignite into cold gold tally-marks, while the looming clock tower gains a fresh doom-mark in the blood-dark sky. The self-writing book hovers high in the wide frame, ash drifting below. Wide 4:3 landscape, bone white and deep black, sacred dread, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Tally of the Damned
text:
Whenever an opponent rolls one or more dice or flips one or more coins, that opponent loses 1 life and you put a doom counter on the Doomsday Clock. The ledger of sins fills itself.
At the beginning of your end step, if there are eight or more doom counters on the Doomsday Clock, each opponent loses 2 life and you gain 2 life.
flavorText: Every wager, every gamble, every lucky toss — all of it goes in the book. The book is very full.
